**Petegem-aan-de-Leie** is a village in the Belgian province of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders") and a borough of [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze"). Petegem-aan-de-Leie is located on the south bank of the river Leie ([Lys](/wiki/Lys_(river) "Lys (river)")). The village today forms a single urban core with that of Deinze itself, which is located just across the river.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petegem-aan-de-Leie&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Old records of Petegem go back to the 12th century. Petegem received a city hallmark in 1293 and consisted of two parts. Petegem-Binnen (Petegem-city) was located just south of the bridge over the Leie (Lys) to Deinze. The church was also located here. Petegem-Buiten consisted of several agricultural settlements in the surrounding country. [Charles the Bold](/wiki/Charles_the_Bold "Charles the Bold") added Petegem-Binnen to the city of Deinze in 1469.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) The parish church of Petegem thus came to stand on the territory of Deinze. De Dries, where the roads from [Gavere](/wiki/Gavere "Gavere") and [Oudenaarde](/wiki/Oudenaarde "Oudenaarde") converged, served as the center of the village.

The Kortrijk-Ghent road formed an important connecting road that ran from west to east through Petegem, along which various taverns and inns were established. In 1839 the [railway line Gent - Kortrijk](/wiki/Belgian_railway_line_75 "Belgian railway line 75") opened parallel to the stone road. Thereafter, industry developed in Petegem.

Petegem continued to grow and in the mid-1960s a new center district was built south of the old center, where a new town hall was built, a new parish church, a swimming pool and a sports hall. This new town hall was used as a police station after the merger, but then fell into disuse when a central police station was built on the Schipdonk Canal. The building was demolished in 2016.

Until 1963 it was simply called Petegem, from that date the suffix -aan-de-Leie was added to the name to distinguish it with [Petegem-aan-de-Schelde](/wiki/Petegem-aan-de-Schelde "Petegem-aan-de-Schelde") in the same province, 15 km south. With the municipal mergers of 1971, Petegem became a sub-municipality of [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze").

Petegem was partly part of the marquisate of Deinze and partly part of the [Drongen Abbey](/wiki/Drongen_Abbey "Drongen Abbey"). Hence, its history runs parallel with that of Deinze, of which it is part since the 1971 merger of municipalities.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petegem-aan-de-Leie&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Sights")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f3/Sint-Martinus-_en_Sint-Antonius_Abt-kerk_-_Petegem_-_Deinze_%281%29.jpg/250px-Sint-Martinus-_en_Sint-Antonius_Abt-kerk_-_Petegem_-_Deinze_%281%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sint-Martinus-_en_Sint-Antonius_Abt-kerk_-_Petegem_-_Deinze_(1).jpg)

[Sint-Martinus-en-Sint-Antonius-Abtkerk](/w/index.php?title=Sint-Martinus-en-Sint-Antonius-Abtkerk&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sint-Martinus-en-Sint-Antonius-Abtkerk (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sint-Martinus-en-Sint-Antonius-Abtkerk "nl:Sint-Martinus-en-Sint-Antonius-Abtkerk")]

* The St. Paul's Church ([Sint-Pauluskerk (Petegem-aan-de-Leie)](/w/index.php?title=Sint-Pauluskerk_(Petegem-aan-de-Leie)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sint-Pauluskerk (Petegem-aan-de-Leie) (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sint-Pauluskerk_(Petegem-aan-de-Leie) "nl:Sint-Pauluskerk (Petegem-aan-de-Leie)")]), the parish church built in 1976 near the new administrative center
* The [Sint-Martinus-en-Sint-Antonius-Abtkerk](/w/index.php?title=Sint-Martinus-en-Sint-Antonius-Abtkerk&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sint-Martinus-en-Sint-Antonius-Abtkerk (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sint-Martinus-en-Sint-Antonius-Abtkerk "nl:Sint-Martinus-en-Sint-Antonius-Abtkerk")], whose history dates back to 1147. It is the oldest parish church in Petegem. However, it is located on the territory of Deinze, just north of the municipal boundary.
* Het Nieuwgoed, a walled farm, protected as a monument in Belgium, with an imposing 15th-century entrance gate[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4)

## Nature & Landscape

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petegem-aan-de-Leie&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Nature & Landscape")]

Petegem-aan-de-Leie is located in [Zandig Vlaanderen](/w/index.php?title=Zandig_Vlaanderen&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zandig Vlaanderen (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zandig_Vlaanderen "nl:Zandig Vlaanderen")] and in the valley of the Leie ([Lys](/wiki/Lys_(river) "Lys (river)")), at an altitude of 10–13 meters. Petegem is highly urbanized and the streams that lead to the Lys are almost all flooded.

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petegem-aan-de-Leie&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Politics")]

Petegem had its own municipal council and mayor until the [municipal merger](/wiki/Fusion_of_the_Belgian_municipalities "Fusion of the Belgian municipalities"). Adolf Haerens was the last mayor of the independent municipality before the 1971 merger.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The mayors of Petegem were:

...

* 1934-1936 : [Julian Filliers](/w/index.php?title=Julian_Filliers&action=edit&redlink=1 "Julian Filliers (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Julian_Filliers "nl:Julian Filliers")]
* 1936-1970 : [Adolf Haerens](/w/index.php?title=Adolf_Haerens&action=edit&redlink=1 "Adolf Haerens (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adolf_Haerens "nl:Adolf Haerens")]

## Infrastructure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petegem-aan-de-Leie&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Infrastructure")]

The municipal swimming pool of Deinze is located in the borough of Petegem.

## Traffic

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petegem-aan-de-Leie&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Traffic")]

There are important roads to and from Deinze: the [N43](/w/index.php?title=N43_(Belgium)&action=edit&redlink=1 "N43 (Belgium) (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/N43_(Belgi%C3%AB) "nl:N43 (België)")] from [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") to [Kortrijk](/wiki/Kortrijk "Kortrijk") and the [N35](/w/index.php?title=N35_(Belgium)&action=edit&redlink=1 "N35 (Belgium) (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/N35_(Belgi%C3%AB) "nl:N35 (België)")] intersect in Petegem-aan-de-Leie.

The [railway line Gent - Kortrijk - Lille](/wiki/Belgian_railway_line_75 "Belgian railway line 75") runs through Petegem, from which the line Deinze - De Panne ([Line 73](/w/index.php?title=Line_73&action=edit&redlink=1 "Line 73 (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spoorlijn_73 "nl:Spoorlijn 73")]) branches off here. The station of Deinze is located on the territory of Petegem.

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petegem-aan-de-Leie&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sports")]

The district is home to the football club [K.F.C. Sparta Petegem](/wiki/K.F.C._Sparta_Petegem "K.F.C. Sparta Petegem"). It played in the national championships for several seasons.

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petegem-aan-de-Leie&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Marc De Buck](/w/index.php?title=Marc_De_Buck&action=edit&redlink=1 "Marc De Buck (page does not exist)")  [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marc_De_Buck "nl:Marc De Buck")], politician (born 1945)
* [Jo De Clercq](/w/index.php?title=Jo_De_Clercq&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jo De Clercq (page does not exist)")  [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jo_De_Clercq "nl:Jo De Clercq")], singer and politician (born 1947)
* [Daniel Janssens](/wiki/Daniel_Janssens "Daniel Janssens"), middle-distance runner (born 1925)
* [Charles Felix Van Quickenborne](/wiki/Charles_Felix_Van_Quickenborne "Charles Felix Van Quickenborne"), founder of [Saint Louis University](/wiki/Saint_Louis_University_(United_States) "Saint Louis University (United States)") in St. Louis, Mo., USA (1788–1837)
